creator: hrothgar.ofstingan@gmail.com
text:
Just as additional feedback, it's strange how the wired memory usage seems to account for the memory not being freed. Despite chrome no longer showing up under top or ps when the process is quit, it does exhibit behaviour like the memory is still reserved for the programme. (Since, restarting the programme from the shell and I get instantanenous reloading of the programme, whereas, starting from a cold restart I wait maybe 1 to 2 seconds before twm prompts me in the UI for where to place the window).

Checking online, this appears to be related to the different way(s) that freebsd can manage memory.

Let me know if I need to send across any more info.
